description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, dark wooden handle with a pronounced curve, designed for ergonomic grip and efficient swinging. The handle appears to be made from a single piece of wood, smoothly finished but retaining some natural grain and texture. The axe head is forged from dark metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a slightly tapered poll at the rear. The blade edge is straight and sharp, suitable for chopping or splitting wood, and the head is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fit. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, giving the tool a utilitarian, functional appearance. The overall design suggests durability and effectiveness for tasks such as woodcutting or general utility work in urban or suburban environments.
description: A sturdy, curved wooden-handled axe with a dark metal wedge-shaped blade, designed for chopping and splitting tasks. The tool has a utilitarian, functional look with no visible branding or decoration.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow, pointed edge of the blade and the alignment of the handle with the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the blade's profile, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Handle-end view showing the rounded butt of the handle and the thickness of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an oval cross-section, smooth finish, ergonomic grip, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a straight cutting edge and a slightly tapered poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fit, with the blade head fitted onto the handle and secured by friction.
